channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: US sends home a Kenyan man held 17 years without charge at Guantanamo military prison
item.description: The U.S. has repatriated a Kenya man held for 17 years without charge at the U.S. military prison in Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New mission for longtime Trump ally and friend Herschel Walker
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has a new game plan for Herschel Walker, naming the former football star and Republican Senate candidate as ambassador to the Bahamas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans butt heads on daylight saving time after Trump directive
item.description: Trump pledged that “The Republican Party will use its best efforts to eliminate Daylight Saving Time"
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Biden says there’s ‘no sense of danger’ from drones
item.description: “Nothing nefarious apparently, but they’re checking it all out,” Biden told reporters about the drones.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: In last-ditch effort, Democrats push to get Julie Su confirmed as labor secretary
item.description: Acting Secretary Julie Su has led the Labor Department for nearly two years, despite never getting a Senate confirmation vote. With time running out, her staunchest supporters haven't given up.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Nevada's 6 Electoral College votes certified for President-elect Donald Trump
item.description: Nevada Republican Party Chairman Michael McDonald officially certified the state's six Electoral College votes for President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday during a gathering on the UNR campus in Reno.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Horsford vows to defend Nevada families as priority for government cuts grows
item.description: Nevada Democratic Rep. Steven Horsford is doing more than just serving as a watchdog on Republican initiatives to cut government bureaucracy -- he's participating in the process.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: RFK Stadium site provision included in federal spending bill, a win for the Commanders and NFL
item.description: The Washington Commanders are another step closer to the possibility of returning to the franchise’s old home in the nation’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Whyno, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Farm state Republicans appear skeptical about RFK Jr amid his quest for HHS confirmation
item.description: Some rural Republicans are hoping Trump HHS secretary pick RFK Jr. will provide some clarification about his stances on farming and food production.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump sues Des Moines Register, pollster for 'election interference' after pre-election poll
item.description: The Register's parent Gannett Co. on Tuesday dismissed the lawsuit as meritless and said it would vigorously defend its First Amendment rights.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Republicans in key red state launch campaign to elect ‘true’ conservatives ahead of Trump return
item.description: Conservatives in the most populous red state in America are launching a new Texas Republican Leadership Fund to elect “true" conservatives to crucial leadership roles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP lawmakers demand Biden appointees who have accepted ENGO jobs to recuse themselves from pending business
item.description: Republicans penned a letter to the Bureau of Land Management calling on appointees who have announced their intent to join ENGOs after the administration to recuse themselves from pending business.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Archivist says Equal Rights Amendment can't be certified as Democrats push Biden to recognize it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In a rare joint statement, the archivist and deputy archivist of the United States said Tuesday that the 1970s-era Equal Rights Amendment cannot be certified without further action by Congress or the courts, as Democrats press President Joe Biden to act unilaterally on its ratifica
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden’s Cabinet officials stand by statements of support as term draws to a close
item.description: With a month left in office, Biden’s Cabinet reaffirms support, stating confidence in his leadership and fitness despite ongoing controversy and debate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jill Biden says she has closed the book on teaching at Northern Virginia Community College
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Jill Biden has closed the book on her teaching career at a Virginia community college. The first lady, who has spent the past 40 years teaching in classrooms, announced Monday that she had taught her final class at Northern Virginia Community College last week.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's transition team eyes expansion of ankle monitors for illegal immigrants not in custody
item.description: The incoming Trump administration is looking at potentially expanding the number of illegal immigrants on GPS monitoring, while also extending the amount of time they are monitored.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kamala Harris tells students to 'stay in the fight' after the election
item.description: The vice president's remarks to students in the Maryland Corps service year program were her most extensive since her concession speech. She did not disclose her own plans for after her term ends, but she spoke about “our shared commitment to the work ahead.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Congress unveils funding deal with more than $100 billion in disaster aid
item.description: Congressional leaders have unveiled legislation that will keep the federal government funded through March 14 and provide more than $100 billion in emergency aid to help states and local communities recover from Hurricanes Helene and Milton and other natural disasters.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump seeks to smooth over concerns over RFK Jr
item.description: “I think he’s going to be much less radical than you would think,” Trump said during a wide-ranging news conference at Mar-a-Lago.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Connolly bests Ocasio-Cortez in key vote to lead Democrats on Oversight panel
item.description: Rep. Gerry Connolly (D-Va.) on Monday claimed the first round of the two-step contest to lead Democrats on the House Oversight and Accountability Committee in the next Congress, besting Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Federal agents, hazmat sent to home of Ohio Secretary of State
item.description: Multiple agencies were sent to Ohio Secretary of State Frank Larose's residence for a reported "security incident.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, NewsNation

item.title: Trump sues Des Moines Register, pollster
item.description: “Selzer’s polling ‘miss’ was not an astonishing coincidence — it was intentional,” the complaint states.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: In disputed Shakopee-area legislative race, voters share what was on uncounted ballots
item.description: While 20 ballots believed to have been discarded before being counted won’t be recovered, some voters who cast those missing ballots shared what was on them. They appear to show Rep. Brad Tabke’s narrow lead would stand up.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump tariff plan threatens to drive up gas prices
item.description: Trump said that on his first day in office, he would place 25 percent tariffs on all Canadian and Mexican goods. The two countries are the leading sources of U.S. oil imports.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Democrats divided over Biden administration response to reported drone sightings
item.description: The Biden administration’s response to mounting public concern over alleged drone sightings in the Northeast is dividing Democrats on Capitol Hill, where some lawmakers are defending the executive assurances that there’s no threat to public safety and others are bashing the various agencies for not
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two charged in connection with Iran-backed drone strike that killed 3 US troops in the Middle East
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Two men, including a dual Iranian American citizen, have been charged with conspiring to export sensitive technology to Iran that was used in a drone attack in Jordan that killed three American troops early this year and injured dozens of other service members, the Justice Department s
item.dc:creator: STEVE LEBLANC, ERIC TUCKER and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge rejects Trump’s bid to toss hush money conviction
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge Monday refused to throw out President-elect Donald Trump’s hush money conviction because of the U.S. Supreme Court's recent ruling on presidential immunity. But the overall future of the historic case remains unclear. Manhattan Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: RFK Jr. meets with senators as questions swirl about Trump's pick to lead health agency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Robert F. Kennedy Jr. swept onto Capitol Hill late Monday as the anti-vaccine health guru from the famous political family reintroduced himself to senators, this time as President-elect Donald Trump's pick to lead the nation's Health and Human Services Department.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Jersey drones not national security risk: White House
item.description: Kirby stressed that “there are lots of drones in the sky” and the “vast majority of them are doing good things.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok asks the Supreme Court for an emergency order to block a U.S. ban unless it’s sold
item.description: A similar plea was filed by content creators who rely on the platform for income and some of TikTok’s more than 170 million users in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Journalists anticipate a renewed hostility toward their work under the incoming Trump administration
item.description: For the press heading into a second Trump administration, there's a balancing act between being prepared and being fearful.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden establishes a national monument for Frances Perkins, the 1st female Cabinet secretary
item.description: President Joe Biden on Monday established a national monument honoring the late FDR-era Labor Secretary Frances Perkins, the first woman to serve in a presidential Cabinet and a driving force behind Franklin D. Roosevelt's New Deal programs after the Great Depression.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, AAMER MADHANI and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams' former chief advisor expects to be indicted: lawyer
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ingrid Lewis-Martin, a former top aide to New York Mayor Eric Adams, expects to be indicted on criminal charges related to alleged improper gifts, her lawyer said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Report documents family separations during 1st Trump term
item.description: A report examining years of family separation policies enacted under the Trump administration is being released Monday by several migrant advocacy nonprofit organizations to remind the public and lawmakers of what transpired, Border Report has learned.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Trump claims a 'massive' mandate, but presidents often overread their victories
item.description: There's a long history of presidents claiming that their win means the will of the people is behind their agendas. But elections are rarely full-throated endorsements of a candidate's every policy.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Pelosi has hip replacement surgery at a US military hospital in Germany after a fall
item.description: Pelosi, 84, “is well on the mend,” said Ian Krager, a spokesman for the California Democrat, in a statement.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: READ: Here is Biden's full list of clemencies
item.description: President Biden early Thursday announced that he is pardoning 39 people and commuting the sentences of nearly 1,500 others, setting a new daily record for clemency with a focus on those who were under home confinement during the COVID-19 pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Balluck, Fox 5
